1.10: the justice of our cause ; that is to say, our just rights, violated by the Chaldeans.
1.16: ; the removal of the sacred vessels from the Temple, vv.
1.18: As I visited the king of Assyria . The Lord visited the king of’ Assyria , not only by the defeat of Sennacherib, but also by the ruin of Nineveh, and of the Assyrian empire.
1.3: A nation , etc.; the Medes and the Persians, who were united in the person of Cyrus.
1.6: On the ninth day . See Jeremiah ,
1.13: ; the deportation of the inhabitants, except for the poor workers, vv.
1.7: The two walls ; that is to say the wall and the forewall. cf.
1.24: The second-class priest ; the first after the high priest, the temple steward, also called prince of priests .
1.5: Against the holy one of Israel ; That is to say, committed against the Lord.
1.21: On the Red Sea . The navigators of the Red Sea, who trade with Idumea, hear the news of its ruin.
1.32: Their camels . These animals are one of the main sources of wealth for nomads.
1.35: My flesh ; That is to say, my children killed.
1.20: there was no need to weigh the bronze , etc. See

Scripture mentions three kings of Damascus with this name, but it also names others with different names, such as Hazael.
1.28: Consecrate the nations against her , etc. See Jeremiah ,

1.26: They will not take from you nor cornerstones , etc. You will not be rebuilt. When the kings of Babylon wanted to rebuild a structure, they first looked at the corner of the monument for the tablets that were hidden there and that recounted the history of the foundation.
1.17: Every man , etc. See for an explanation of this verse, Jeremiah
1.45: See for an explanation of these two verses, Jeremiah ,
1.36: I will dry up its sea and make its spring run dry . This prophecy was fulfilled literally when Cyrus, who accompanied his uncle Darius, diverted the waters of the Euphrates that flowed through Babylon, thus drying up the riverbed. His sea here refers to the Euphrates.
1.43: The son of man . See, regarding this expression, Jeremiah ,
1.38: these scarecrows ; monstrous idols that inspire terror — drought on its waters . Babylonia owed its fertility to the Euphrates and the many canals that had been diverted from it. Since its canals dried up, it has been barren.
1.39: This prophecy was fulfilled during the reign of Cyrus.
1.55: ), and they continued to live in sobriety and poverty.
1.46: People of Chamos ; That is, worshippers of Hamos. See verse
1.47: But I will bring back , etc. After the Babylonian captivity, as is generally believed, the Moabites, like the Jews, returned to their homeland. Even in the time of Jesus Christ, they had their own country, but later they were absorbed into the Arab tribes.
1.50: Escaped from the sword , Jews, who were spared by the victor.
1.59: Saraïas, son of Nerias , was supposed to be a brother of Baruch, secretary to Jeremiah.

1.52: . ― The book of Jeremiah ends with a concluding chapter, showing how all the prophecies it contains about the holy city were fulfilled; it recounts the capture of Jerusalem by Nebuchadnezzar, after a two-year siege, vv.

2.1: The word of the Lord came to me in these terms:
2.2: Go and proclaim in the hearing of Jerusalem, saying: Thus says the Lord: I have remembered the piety of your youth, the love of your betrothal, when you followed me in the wilderness, in a land not sown.
2.3: Israel was the Lord’s holy thing, the firstfruits of his harvest; whoever ate of it was guilty, and disaster befell him, declares the Lord.,
2.4: Listen to the word of the Lord, you house of Jacob and all you families of the house of Israel:
2.5: Thus says the Lord: What iniquity did your fathers find in me, that they strayed from me, that they followed vanity and became vanity themselves?
2.6: They did not say, “Where is the Lord, who brought us up out of the land of Egypt, who led us through the desert, through the dry and broken land, through the land of drought and the shadow of death, through the land where no man passes through and where no one lives?”
2.7: And I brought you into a land like an orchard, to eat its fruits and its bounty, and once you entered, you defiled my land and made my inheritance an abomination.
2.8: The priests did not say, “Where is the Lord?” The custodians of the law did not know me. The shepherds were unfaithful to me, and the prophets prophesied by Baal and followed those who are of no help.
2.9: Therefore, I will plead against you again. This is the Lord's oracle, and I will plead against your children's children.
2.10: Go to the islands of Cethim and look, send to Cedar and observe carefully and see if there is anything similar there.
2.11: Does a nation change its gods? And yet they are not gods. And my people have exchanged their glory for that which is worthless.
2.12: Be astonished, O heavens, and utterly amazed, declares the Lord.,
2.13: For my people have done two evils: they have forsaken me, the spring of living water, and have dug their own cisterns, broken cisterns that cannot hold water.
2.14: Is Israel a slave? Was he born of a slave in the house? Why then is he treated like plunder?
2.15: Against him the lion cubs roar, they raise their cries and they devastate his country. His cities are burned, without inhabitants.
2.16: Even the sons of Noph and Taphnes shave your head.
2.17: Is this not happening to you because you abandoned the Lord your God when he was guiding you in the way?
2.18: And now what do you have to do on the road to Egypt, to go and drink the water of the Nile, and what do you have to do on the road to Assyria, to go and drink the water of the river?
2.19: Your impiety chastises you, and your rebellions punish you. Know therefore and see how evil and bitter it is that you have forsaken the Lord your God and have no fear of me. This is the oracle of the Lord God of hosts.
2.20: For long ago you broke your yoke, you tore off your bonds, and you said, “I will serve no more.” For on every high hill and under your green tree you lay about like a prostitute.
2.21: And I had planted you like a fine vine, entirely from a single, pure stock. How then did you turn into bastard branches of a foreign vine for me?
2.22: Yes, even if you were to wash yourself with lye and lavish potash, your iniquity would be a stain before me. This is the oracle of the Lord God.
2.23: How can you say: I have not defiled myself, I have not gone after the Baals? See the traces of your footsteps in the Valley, acknowledge what you have done. Light camel, crossing her steps in every direction,
2.24: The wild ass, accustomed to the desert, in the ardor of her passion, breathes in the air: who will prevent her from satisfying her desire? None of those who seek her need tire themselves, they find her in her month.
2.25: Take care that your foot does not become bare and your throat does not become parched. But you say: It's useless. No, for I love strangers and I will go after them.
2.26: Just as a thief caught in the act is put to shame, so the house of Israel was confounded, along with their kings, their leaders, their priests, and their prophets.,
2.27: who say to the wood: You are my father, and to the stone: You gave birth to me, for they have turned their backs on me and not their faces, and in the time of their misfortune they say: Arise and save us.
2.28: Where are the gods you have made for yourself? Let them rise up, if they can save you in your time of trouble. For as numerous as your cities are your gods, O Judah.
2.29: Why are you pleading against me? You have all been unfaithful to me, declares the Lord.,
2.30: It is in vain that I have struck your sons, they have not learned anything from it, your sword has devoured your prophets, like a destructive lion.
2.31: What kind of people are you? Consider the word of the Lord: Have I been a desert to Israel, a land of thick darkness? Why did my people say, “We are free; we will not return to you”?
2.32: Does a virgin forget her jewelry, or a bride her sash? Yet my people have forgotten me for countless days.
2.33: You know how to arrange your ways to seek love. For this, even with crime you make your ways familiar.
2.34: Even on the hems of your clothes, we find the blood of poor innocents; you did not catch them breaking and entering, but you killed them for all these things.
2.35: And you say: Yes, I am innocent, surely his anger has turned away from me. Here I am to put you on trial for what you say: I have not sinned.
2.36: How hasty you are in changing your ways! You will be confounded by Egypt, as you were by Assyria.
2.37: From there you will also return with your hands on your head, for the Lord has rejected those in whom you place your trust, and you will not succeed with them.

3.1: It is said: If a man divorces his wife and she becomes another man's wife, will he return to her again? Would not that land be defiled? But you have played the harlot with many lovers, and yet you will return to me. This is the Lord's oracle.
3.2: Lift up your eyes to the heights and look: where have you not been defiled? You sat for them on the roads, like an Arab in the desert. And you defiled the land with your prostitution and your wickedness.
3.3: The autumn showers were held back, the spring rains were lacking. But you had the composure of a courtesan, you refused to blush.
3.4: And now, don't you say to me: My father, O you, friend of my youth. Will he always be angry,
3.5: Will he keep his wrath forever? That's what you say, and you commit the crime and you consummate it.
3.6: The Lord said to me in the days of King Josiah: Have you seen what faithless Israel has done? She has gone up on every high mountain and under every green tree and has prostituted herself there.
3.7: And I said, "After she has done all these things, she will return to me." But she did not return. And her sister, treacherous Judah, saw this.
3.8: And I saw that because of all her adulteries, I divorced faithless Israel and gave her her certificate of divorce, and her sister Judah the treacherous was not afraid, and she too went and prostituted herself.
3.9: By her noisy immodesty, she defiled the land and committed adultery with wood and stone.
3.10: And besides all this, her sister, treacherous Judah, did not return to me with all her heart, but with lies, declares the Lord.,
3.11: And the Lord said to me: “Faithless Israel has shown itself to be righteous, compared to treacherous Judah.”.
3.12: Go and proclaim these words toward the north and say: Return, faithless Israel. This is the Lord’s declaration: I will not be angry with you, for I am merciful. This is the Lord’s declaration: I will not retain my anger forever.
3.13: Only acknowledge your sin, for you have been unfaithful to the Lord your God, and you have scattered your steps to foreigners under your green tree, and you have not listened to my voice, declares the Lord.,
3.14: Return, faithless sons, declares the Lord, for I am your master and I will take you, one from a town and two from a family, and I will bring you to Zion.
3.15: And I will give you shepherds after my own heart, who will lead you with understanding and wisdom.
3.16: And when you have multiplied and prospered in the land in those days, declares the Lord, then people will no longer call it the ark of the covenant of the Lord. It will no longer be remembered, nor will it be mourned, nor will another one be made.
3.17: At that time Jerusalem will be called the throne of the Lord, and all nations will gather in Jerusalem in the name of the Lord, and they will no longer follow the stubbornness of their evil hearts.
3.18: In those days the house of Judah will walk with the house of Israel, and they will come together from the land of the north to the land that I gave as an inheritance to your fathers.
3.19: And I said, “How shall I place you among my children? And shall I give you a pleasant land, the most beautiful jewel of the nations, as an inheritance?” And I said, “You shall call me ‘My Father,’ and you shall never cease to follow me.”.
3.20: But as a woman betrays her lover, so you have been unfaithful to me, house of Israel, declares the Lord,
3.21: A cry is heard on the high places, the weeping of the children of Israel pleading for mercy because they have perverted their way, forgotten the Lord their God.
3.22: Return, faithless sons, and I will heal your faithlessness. Here we are, we are coming to you, for you are the Lord our God.
3.23: Yes, it was in vain that the tumult of idolatrous festivals resounded on the heights, on the mountains. Yes, it is in the Lord our God that the salvation of Israel lies.
3.24: The shame of idols has devoured from our youth the product of the labor of our fathers, their sheep and their oxen, their sons and their daughters.
3.25: Let us lie down in our shame and let our reproach cover us, for it is against the Lord our God that we have sinned, we and our fathers, from our youth to this day, and we have not obeyed the voice of the Lord our God.

4.1: If you wish to return, Israel, declares the Lord, return to me. And if you remove your abominations from before me, you will no longer be a wanderer.
4.2: And if you swear that the Lord lives, in truth, in uprightness, and in justice, the nations will say, “Blessed are they in him,” and will glory in him.
4.3: For thus says the Lord to the men of Judah and Jerusalem: Clear your fallow land and do not sow among thorns.
4.4: Circumcise yourselves for the Lord and remove the foreskins of your hearts, you men of Judah and inhabitants of Jerusalem, lest my wrath break out like fire and consume, with no one to quench it, because of the wickedness of your deeds.
4.5: Publish in Judah and proclaim in Jerusalem. Speak, sound the trumpet throughout the land. Cry aloud and say: Assemble yourselves and let us go to the fortified cities.
4.6: Raise a banner toward Zion, save yourselves, do not stop, for I am bringing calamity and great disaster from the north.
4.7: A lion leaps from its thicket, and a destroyer of nations has broken tent and left its place, to make your land a desert. Your cities will be desolate, without inhabitants.
4.8: Therefore put on sackcloth, weep and wail, for the fire of the Lord’s wrath has not turned away from us.
4.9: On that day, declares the Lord, the hearts of the king and princes will fail. The priests will be dismayed and the prophets astonished.
4.10: And I said: Ah, Lord God, you have deceived this people and Jerusalem, saying: You will have peace when the sword will strike them dead.
4.11: At that time it will be said to this people and to Jerusalem: A scorching wind is coming from the desert hills on the road that leads to the daughter of my people, not to winnow, nor to cleanse.
4.12: A wind stronger than the one that blows away the straw is coming towards me. Now, in my turn, I will pronounce their sentence.
4.13: Behold, he rises like the clouds, his chariots like a hurricane, his horses swifter than eagles. Woe to us, for we are lost.
4.14: Purify your heart from wickedness, Jerusalem, so that you may be saved; how long will your evil thoughts remain in your heart?
4.15: For a voice from Dan announces it, it proclaims disaster from the mountain of Ephraim.
4.16: Make it known to the nations, announce to them the misfortune of Jerusalem. Besiegers are coming from a distant land, they are raising their cries against the cities of Judah.
4.17: Like watchmen of the fields, they surround Jerusalem, for she has rebelled against me, declares the Lord.,
4.18: This is what your conduct and your criminal acts have earned you; this is the fruit of your wickedness, and it is bitter. Yes, it reaches to the heart.
4.19: My bowels, my bowels, I suffer in the very depths of my heart. My heart is in turmoil, I cannot remain silent. For you hear, O my soul, the sound of the trumpet, the war cry.
4.20: Ruin upon ruin is reported, for the whole country is ravaged. All at once my tents are destroyed, in an instant my pavilions.
4.21: How long will I see the banner, hear the sound of the trumpet?
4.22: My people are foolish. They do not know me. They are senseless children, lacking understanding; they are skilled at doing evil, but do not know how to do good.
4.23: I look at the earth and behold, it is formless and empty; the heavens and their light have vanished.
4.24: I look at the mountains and behold, they are shaken and all the hills are tottering.
4.25: I look, and behold, there is no man left, and all the birds of the air have fled.
4.26: I look, and behold, the orchard has become a desert, and all its cities are destroyed before the Lord, before the fire of his anger.
4.27: For thus says the Lord: The whole land shall be devastated, yet I will not destroy it entirely.
4.28: Because of this, the earth mourns and the heavens above are darkened, because I have spoken and resolved, and I do not repent and I will not turn back.
4.29: At the sound of the rider and the archer's voice, the whole city fled. They entered the woods, climbed the rocks, all the cities were abandoned, there were no more inhabitants.
4.30: And you, devastated, what will you do? Even if you were to dress yourself in purple, and adorn yourself with ornaments of gold, and line your eyes with rouge, it would be in vain that you would make yourself beautiful: your lovers disdain you, it is your life they seek.
4.31: For I hear a voice like that of a woman in labor, cries of anguish like that of a woman giving birth for the first time, the voice of the daughter of Zion, who groans and stretches out her hands: Woe is me, for my soul is overcome by the blows of the murderers.

5.1: Go through the streets of Jerusalem and look around, inquire, search in its public squares if you can find a man there who practices justice and seeks faithfulness, and I will show mercy to the city.
5.2: Even when they say, "As the Lord lives," they swear by lies.
5.3: Lord, do not your eyes seek faithfulness? You struck them, but they felt no pain; you destroyed them, but they refused to be instructed; they hardened their faces more than rock; they refused to return.
5.4: And I said, “These are only the little ones. They act foolishly, for they do not know the way of the Lord, the law of their God.”.
5.5: So I will go to the great ones and speak to them, for they know the way of the Lord, the law of their God. But they too have all together broken the yoke, shattered the bonds.
5.6: That is why the lion of the forest has struck them, the wolf of the desert ravages them, the panther lies in wait outside their cities, every man who leaves them is torn apart because their transgressions are numerous and their revolts have increased.
5.7: Why should I show you mercy? Your sons have forsaken me and swear by what is not God. I have satisfied their hunger and thirst, yet they have committed adultery; they go in droves to the house of the prostitute.
5.8: Well-fed, vagabond stallions, each of them neighs at his neighbor's wife.
5.9: And I would not punish them for these crimes? The Lord's oracle, and against a nation like that, I would not take vengeance?
5.10: Climb its walls and destroy it, but not entirely. Remove its branches, for they do not belong to the Lord.
5.11: For they have been utterly faithless to me, the house of Israel and the house of Judah, declares the Lord,
5.12: They denied the Lord and said, "He is not, and no harm will come upon us; we will not see the sword or famine.".
5.13: The prophets are but wind and no one speaks through them: let it be done to them so.
5.14: Therefore this is what the Lord, the God of hosts, says: Because you speak this word, behold, I put my word in your mouth like fire, and this people shall be like wood, and this fire shall consume them.
5.15: Here I am bringing against you a nation from afar, the house of Israel, declares the Lord. It is a strong nation, it is an ancient nation, a nation whose language you do not know and whose words you do not understand.
5.16: His quiver is an open sepulcher; they are all heroes.
5.17: It will devour your harvest and your bread, it will devour your sons and your daughters, it will devour your sheep and your cattle, it will devour your vine and your fig tree, it will destroy with the sword your fortified cities in which you trust.
5.18: But even in those days, declares the Lord, I will not utterly destroy you.
5.19: And when you say, “Why has the Lord our God done all these things to us?” you shall say to them, “Just as you abandoned me to serve a foreign god in your own land, so you will serve foreigners in a land that is not yours.”.
5.20: Announce this in the house of Jacob and publish it in Judah, saying:
5.21: Listen to this, you foolish and heartless people. They have eyes but do not see, and ears but do not hear.
5.22: Will you not fear me, declares the Lord? Will you not tremble before me, I who have set the sand as a boundary for the sea, an eternal barrier it cannot cross? Its waves may surge, but they cannot prevail; they may roar, but they cannot overtake it.
5.23: But this people has an unruly and rebellious heart; they withdraw and leave.
5.24: They do not say in their hearts: Let us fear the Lord our God, who gives the early rain and the latter rain in its season, and who keeps for us the weeks appointed for harvest.
5.25: Your iniquities have disrupted this order; your sins deprive you of these blessings.
5.26: For there are wicked people among me; they lie in wait like a bird-catcher who crouches; they set traps and catch men.
5.27: As a cage is full of birds, so their houses are full of deceit, so they become powerful and rich.
5.28: They grow fat and gleam. They even exceed the bounds of evil; they do not do justice to the orphan, and they prosper. They do not defend the unfortunate.
5.29: And I will not punish these crimes? The Lord declares. And I will not take revenge on a nation like that?
5.30: Abominable, horrible things are happening in the country.
5.31: The prophets prophesy lies, and the priests rule in agreement with them. And my people love him so. And what will you do in the end?

6.1: Flee, children of Benjamin, from the midst of Jerusalem! Blow the trumpet in Tekuah and raise signals in Beth-carem! For disaster is coming from the north, and great calamity!.
6.2: The beautiful and voluptuous one, I destroy her, the daughter of Zion.
6.3: Shepherds with their flocks will come to it, they will pitch their tents around it, each one will graze in his own area.
6.4: Begin the fight against her: rise up, let us mount the assault at high noon. Woe to us, for the day is waning, the shadows of evening are lengthening.
6.5: Rise up, let us climb by night and destroy his palace.
6.6: For thus says the Lord of hosts: Cut down its trees, raise siege ramps against Jerusalem, for it is the city to be punished, there is nothing but injustice within it.
6.7: As a well pours forth its waters, so she pours forth her wickedness. Violence and ruin are heard there; wounds and injuries are constantly before my face.
6.8: Amend yourself, Jerusalem, lest my soul depart from you, lest I make of you a desert, an uninhabited land.
6.9: Thus says the Lord of hosts: The remnant of Israel shall be gleaned like a vine, put in and put out again, as the grape-gatherer does to the branches.
6.10: To whom can I speak, to whom can I plead so that he may hear me? Behold, their ears are uncircumcised, and they cannot heed. Behold, the word of the Lord has become to them a reproach, and they take no pleasure in it.
6.11: But I am filled with the wrath of the Lord; I am weary of holding it back. Pour it out on the child in the street and on the gathering of young men, for husband and wife shall all be taken, as well as the old man and the man laden with years.
6.12: Their houses will pass to others, their fields and their wives as well, for I will stretch out my hand against the inhabitants of the land, declares the Lord.,
6.13: For from the smallest to the greatest, all engage in plunder, and from the prophet to the priest, all practice lying.
6.14: They treat lightly the wound of the daughter of my people, saying, "Peace, peace," when there is no peace.
6.15: They will be put to shame, for they have committed abominations, but they no longer even know how to blush or feel ashamed. Therefore they will fall among those who fall; they will be brought low on the day I visit them, says the Lord.
6.16: Thus says the Lord: Stand at the roads and see, inquire of the ancient paths, what is the way of salvation? And walk in it and you will find rest for your souls. But they answered: We will not walk in it.
6.17: I have posted watchmen near you: Listen carefully to the sound of the trumpet. But they replied: We will not listen.
6.18: Therefore, listen, you nations, and know, you assembly of peoples, what will happen to them,
6.19: Earth, listen: Behold, I am bringing disaster upon this people, the fruit of their thoughts, for they have not heeded my words and have rejected my law.
6.20: What to me is incense from Sheba or precious reeds from a distant land? Your burnt offerings do not please me, your sacrifices are not acceptable to me.
6.21: Therefore this is what the Lord says: “I am placing stumbling blocks before this people; fathers and sons will stumble over them, and both inhabitant and neighbor will perish.”.
6.22: Thus says the Lord: Behold, a people will come from the land of the north, a great nation will arise from the ends of the earth.
6.23: They wield the bow and the javelin, they are cruel and without pity, their voice roars like the sea, they are mounted on horses, ready to fight as one man, against you, daughter of Zion.
6.24: At the news of their approach, our hands went weak, anguish seized us, the pains of a woman giving birth.
6.25: Do not go out into the fields, do not go on the roads, for the enemy holds the sword and terror reigns all around.
6.26: Daughter of my people, gird yourself with the hair shirt, roll in the ashes, mourn as for an only son, make bitter lamentations for suddenly the destroyer comes upon us.
6.27: I have appointed you among my people as a tester, a fortress, so that you may know and test their ways.
6.28: They are all rebels among rebels, they spread slander, they are made of copper and iron, they are all corrupt.
6.29: The bellows have become prey to the fire, the lead is exhausted, in vain we purify, we purify, the wicked do not detach themselves.
6.30: It will be called junk money because the Lord has discarded it.

7.1: The word that came to Jeremiah from the Lord, in these words:
7.2: Stand at the gate of the house of the Lord and there pronounce this word and say: Hear the word of the Lord, all you men of Judah, who enter through these gates to worship the Lord.
7.3: Thus says the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel: Amend your ways and your works and I will make you dwell in this place.
7.4: Do not trust the lying words of those who say, "This is the temple of the Lord, the temple of the Lord, the temple of the Lord.".
7.5: But if you improve your ways and your works, if you faithfully administer justice between one man and his neighbor.
7.6: If you do not oppress the foreigner, the orphan, and the widow, if you do not shed innocent blood in this place, and if you do not go to other gods, to your own misfortune:
7.7: Then I will make you dwell in this place, in the land that I gave to your fathers from ancient times and forever.
7.8: But now you are relying on false words, which do you no good.
7.9: What. Stealing, killing, committing adultery, swearing falsely, worshipping Baal and going after other gods that you do not know.
7.10: And you come, you present yourselves before me, in this house which is called by my name, and you say, 'We shall escape.' And it is in order to commit all these abominations.
7.11: Is this house, which bears my name, a den of robbers in your eyes? I too have seen it, declares the Lord.,
7.12: Go now to my dwelling place which was in Shiloh, where I once made my name dwell, and see how I have dealt with it, because of the wickedness of my people Israel.
7.13: And now, since you have done all these things, declares the Lord, and I have spoken to you again and again, and you have not listened, and I have called you, and you have not answered,
7.14: I will do to this house, which bears my name and in which you place your trust, and to this place which I have given to you and your ancestors, as I did to Shiloh.
7.15: and I will cast you out of my sight, as I cast out all your brothers, all the descendants of Ephraim.
7.16: And you, do not intercede for this people, do not raise up a complaint or a prayer for them, and do not urge me, for I will not listen to you.
7.17: Do you not see what they are doing in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem?
7.18: The sons gather wood, the fathers light the fire, the women knead dough to make cakes for the queen of heaven and to pour libations to other gods, in order to offend me.
7.19: Is it me they are offending, declares the Lord, or are they not offending themselves, to the shame of their faces?
7.20: Therefore this is what the Lord God says: My anger and my wrath will be poured out on this place, on people and beasts, on the trees of the field and on the fruit of the ground; it will burn and not be quenched.
7.21: Thus says the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel: Add your burnt offerings to your sacrifices and eat the flesh,
7.22: for I did not speak to your fathers nor did I command them anything concerning burnt offerings and sacrifices on the day I brought them out of the land of Egypt.
7.23: But as for the commandment that I gave them, I said to them: Obey my voice and I will be your God and you shall be my people; walk in all the ways that I will prescribe to you, so that you may prosper.
7.24: But they did not listen and they did not pay attention and they walked according to their advice, according to the hardening of their evil heart, they went backwards and not forwards.
7.25: From the day your ancestors left the land of Egypt until this day, I have sent you all my servants the prophets, sending them out every morning
7.26: But they did not listen to me, they did not pay attention, they stiffened their necks, they did worse than their fathers.
7.27: You will tell them all these things, they will not listen to you; you will call them, they will not answer you.
7.28: Then you will say to them: This is the nation that did not listen to the voice of the Lord their God and did not accept instruction; faithfulness has perished, it has disappeared from his mouth.
7.29: Shave your hair and throw it away and raise a lament on the heights, for the Lord has scorned and rejected the race that is the object of his anger.
7.30: For the children of Judah have done what is evil in my sight, declares the Lord; they have set up their abominations in the house that bears my name, to defile it.
7.31: and they built the high places of Topheth in the valley of the son of Hinnom, to burn their sons and daughters in the fire, which I had not commanded and which had not entered my mind.
7.32: Therefore, behold, the days are coming, declares the Lord, when it will no longer be called Topheth or Valley of the son of Hinnom, but Valley of Slaughter, and where burial will take place in Topheth, for lack of space.
7.33: And the corpses of this people will be food for the birds of the air and the beasts of the earth, with no one to drive them away.
7.34: And I will put an end to the sounds of joy and gladness in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem, the singing of bridegroom and the singing of bride, for the land will have become a desert.

8.1: At that time, declares the Lord, the bones of the kings of Judah, the bones of its princes, the bones of the priests, the bones of the prophets, and the bones of the inhabitants of Jerusalem will be brought out of their tombs.
8.2: They shall be spread out before the sun and the moon and all the host of heaven, which they loved and served, which they followed, which they consulted and worshiped. These bones shall not be gathered or buried, but shall be used as fertilizer on the face of the earth.
8.3: And death will be preferred to life by all who remain of this wicked race, in all the places where I have driven them, declares the Lord of hosts.
8.4: Tell them: This is what the Lord says: Do we fall and not get up again? Do we wander and not return?
8.5: Why then does this people of Jerusalem go astray continually? They cling tightly to falsehood; they refuse to return.
8.6: I paid attention and listened: they do not speak as they ought, no one repents of their wickedness, saying: What have I done? They all resume their course, like a horse that rushes into battle.
8.7: Even the stork in the air knows its season, the turtledove, the swallow and the crane, observe the time of their return, but my people do not know the law of the Lord.
8.8: How can you say, “We are wise, and the law of the Lord is with us”? Look, the lying style of the scribes has made it a lie.
8.9: The wise are confounded, dismayed, and taken aback; behold, they have rejected the word of the Lord, and what wisdom do they have?
8.10: That is why I will give their wives to others and their fields to other owners, for from the least to the greatest, all engage in plunder, and from the prophet to the priest, all practice falsehood.
8.11: They treat lightly the wound of the daughter of my people, saying, "Peace, peace," when there is no peace.
8.12: They will be confounded, for they have committed abominations. But they no longer even know how to blush, nor do they know shame. Therefore they will fall among those who fall; they will be cast down on the day I visit them, says the Lord.
8.13: I will gather them up and carry them away, declares the Lord. There will be no more grapes on the vine, nor figs on the fig tree, and even the leaves will wither. And I have given them people who will invade their land.
8.14: Why are we sitting here? Gather together and let us go to the fortified cities and there we shall perish, for the Lord our God is causing us to perish and giving us poisoned water to drink, because we have sinned against the Lord.
8.15: We were waiting for peace and there is nothing good, for a time of healing and here is terror.
8.16: From Dan, we hear the snorting of his horses, the sound of the neighing of his steeds, the whole earth trembles, they are coming, they are devouring the land and everything in it, the city and its inhabitants.
8.17: For behold, I am sending serpents among you, asps against which there is no enchantment; they will bite you, declares the Lord.,
8.18: Oh, my consolation in my sorrow. My heart yearns within me.
8.19: Behold, the cry of distress of the daughter of my people comes to me from a distant land: Is the Lord no longer in Zion? Is her king no longer in her midst? Why have they provoked me to anger with their idols, with the vanities of the foreigner?
8.20: The harvest is over, the reaping is finished, and we are not delivered.
8.21: I am grieved by the grieving of the daughter of my people, I am in mourning, terror has seized me.
8.22: Is there no more balm of Gilead, is there no longer a physician there? Why then has no bandage been put on the daughter of my people?
8.23: Who will turn my head into waters and my eyes into a spring of tears, so that I may weep night and day for the slain of the daughter of my people?

9.1: Who will give me shelter in the desert for travelers? I will abandon my people, I will withdraw from them because they are all adulterers, an assembly of unbelievers.
9.2: They bend their tongues like bows to shoot lies; they are not powerful in the land by the truth, for they go from sin to sin and do not know me, declares the Lord.
9.3: Each of you should be wary of your friends and trust no brother, for every brother only supplants and every friend goes away spreading slander.
9.4: They deceive one another, they do not tell the truth, they train their tongues to lie, they study to do evil.
9.5: You dwell in the midst of bad faith; it is through bad faith that they refuse to know me, declares the Lord.
9.6: Therefore this is what the Lord of hosts says: I will refine them and test them, for what else can I do with the daughter of my people?
9.7: Their tongue is a deadly dart, uttering only lies. With their mouths they say, "Peace to your neighbor," but in their hearts they lay traps for him.
9.8: And I would not punish them for all these crimes? The Lord declares. And I would not take revenge on a nation like that?
9.9: On the mountains I will raise a lamentation and a mourning, on the desert pastures a song of mourning. For they are burned up so that no one passes through them, the sound of herds is no longer heard; from the birds of the air to the cattle, all have fled, have vanished.
9.10: And I will make Jerusalem a heap of stones, a haunt of jackals, and I will make the cities of Judah a desolate place, where no one lives.
9.11: Who is the wise man who will understand these things, the one to whom the mouth of the Lord has spoken, so that he may declare them? Why is the land destroyed, burned like a desert where no one passes through?
9.12: And the Lord said: It is because they have forsaken my law, which I had set before them, that they have not listened to my voice and have not followed it.
9.13: But they walked according to the stubbornness of their hearts and after the Baals, whom their fathers had taught them.
9.14: Therefore this is what the Lord, the God of Israel, says: “I am going to feed this people with wormwood and give them poisoned water to drink.”.
9.15: I will scatter them among nations that neither they nor their fathers have known, and I will send the sword against them until I have exterminated them.
9.16: Thus says the Lord of hosts: Consider ordering the mourners and let them come. Send to the most skilled and let them come.
9.17: Let them hasten, let them sing lamentations over us, let tears flow from our eyes and let tears stream from our eyelids.
9.18: For a sound of lamentation has been heard in Zion: How desolate we are, covered with shame, to the point of abandoning the land, because our dwellings have been torn down!
9.19: Women, listen to the word of the Lord and let your ears receive the word from his mouth. Teach your daughters a lament, and each of your companions a song of mourning.
9.20: For death has come up through our windows and entered our palaces, to make the street child and the young people of the public squares disappear.
9.21: Speak: Thus oracle of the Lord: The corpse of man shall fall like dung on a field and like sheaves behind the reaper, with no one to gather it up.
9.22: Thus says the Lord: Let not the wise boast of their wisdom, let not the strong boast of their strength, let not the rich boast of their riches.
9.23: But let him who boasts boast in this: that he understands and knows me. For I am the Lord, who exercises mercy, justice, and righteousness on earth, for in these things I delight, declares the Lord.,
9.24: “The days are coming,” declares the Lord, “when I will punish everyone circumcised along with the uncircumcised.”
9.25: Egypt, Judah, Edom, the Ammonites, Moab, and all those who shave their temples, who live in the desert, for all the nations are uncircumcised and all the house of Israel is uncircumcised in heart.

10.1: Listen to the word that the Lord speaks to you, house of Israel:
10.2: Thus says the Lord: Do not learn the ways of the nations, nor be terrified at the signs of heaven, as the nations are terrified at them,
10.3: For the customs of the nations are but vanity; they are wood cut from the forest, the work of the hand of the sculptor, fashioned with the chisel,
10.4: which is decorated with silver and gold. It is fixed with nails using hammer blows, so that it does not move.
10.5: These gods are like a pillar made on a lathe; they are carried, they do not walk. Do not fear them: they do no harm, nor can they do any good.
10.6: There is no one like you, Lord; you are great, and your name is great in power.
10.7: Who would not fear you, King of the nations? It is to you that fear is due. For among all the wise men of the nations and in all their kingdoms, there is none like you.
10.8: Together they are stupid and crazy, a teaching of vanity, it's all nonsense.
10.9: Beaten silver brought from Tharsis and gold from Ophaz, the work of sculpture and goldsmith's hand. They are adorned with purple and red, all of them the work of artists.
10.10: But the Lord is truly God; he is a living God and an eternal King; at his anger the earth trembles and the nations cannot endure his wrath.
10.11: This is how you shall speak to them: The gods who did not make heaven and earth shall be exterminated from the earth and from under heaven.
10.12: He made the earth by his power, established the world by his wisdom and understanding, and stretched out the heavens.
10.13: At his voice, the waters gather in the heavens, he makes the clouds rise from the ends of the earth, he makes the lightning flash from which the downpour springs, and he makes the wind come out of its reservoirs.
10.14: Every man is stupid, senseless, every craftsman is ashamed of his idol, for his cast image is nothing but a lie, there is no breath in it.
10.15: It is vanity, a work of deception; on the day of their punishment, it will perish.
10.16: This is not Jacob's portion, for He formed the universe and Israel is the tribe of His inheritance, His name is the Lord of hosts.
10.17: Pick up your luggage from the ground, you who are under siege.
10.18: For thus says the Lord: Behold, this time I will hurl the inhabitants of the land far away, I will press them close, so that the enemy may overtake them.
10.19: Woe to me because of my bruise! My wound is painful, but I say: Yes, this is my pain and I will bear it.
10.20: My tent is devastated, all my ropes are broken, my sons have left me, they are no more, I have no one left to straighten my tent, raise my flags.
10.21: Ah. The pastors are stupid, they did not seek the Lord, therefore they did not prosper and all their flock was scattered.
10.22: A noise, a rumor. Behold, it is coming, a great tumult is coming from the land of the north, to make the cities of Judah a desert, a haunt of jackals.
10.23: I know, O Lord, that man does not have his own way, nor does the man who walks direct his own steps.
10.24: Punish me, Lord, but according to justice and not in your anger, to reduce me to nothing.
10.25: Pour out your wrath on the nations that do not know you, on the peoples who do not call on your name, for they have devoured Jacob, they have devoured him, they are finishing him off and they are laying waste his dwelling place.

11.1: The word that came to Jeremiah from the Lord, in these words:
11.2: Hear the words of this covenant and speak to the men of Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem.
11.3: And you shall say to them: Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel: Cursed be the man who does not obey the words of this covenant,
11.4: that I commanded your fathers on the day I brought them out of the land of Egypt, out of the iron-smelting furnace, saying to them, Obey my voice and do these things, according to all that I command you, and you shall be my people and I will be your God,
11.5: so that I may fulfill the oath I swore to your fathers to give them a land flowing with milk and honey, as it is today. And I answered, “Yes, Lord.”.
11.6: And the Lord said to me, “Proclaim all these words in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem, saying, ‘Hear the words of this covenant and put them into practice.’”.
11.7: For I have earnestly warned your fathers from the day I brought them up out of the land of Egypt until this day, I have continually warned them, saying: Obey my voice.
11.8: And they neither listened nor paid attention; each of them walked according to the stubbornness of their evil heart. And I carried out upon them all the words of this covenant, which I had commanded them to observe, and which they did not observe.
11.9: The Lord said to me: A conspiracy has been found among the men of Judah and among the inhabitants of Jerusalem.
11.10: They have returned to the iniquities of their first fathers, who refused to listen to my words, and they have gone after other gods to serve them. The house of Israel and the house of Judah have broken my covenant that I made with their fathers.
11.11: Therefore this is what the Lord says: “I am going to bring disasters on them from which they cannot escape, and though they cry out to me, I will not listen to them.”.
11.12: And the cities of Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem will go to invoke the gods to whom they offer incense, but these gods will certainly not save them in the time of their calamity.
11.13: For as numerous as your cities are your gods, O Judah, and as numerous as the streets of Jerusalem are the altars you have erected to an infamous idol, the altars you have erected to incense Baal.
11.14: And you, do not intercede for this people and do not raise up supplication or prayer on their behalf, for I will not listen when they call upon me in the time of their calamity.
11.15: What does my beloved have to do in my house? Deceit? Will vows and sacred flesh take away your sorrows, so that you may give yourself over to joy?
11.16: A green olive tree, laden with beautiful fruit: that was the name the Lord had given you. With a great crash, he set it on fire, and its branches were broken.
11.17: The Lord of hosts, who had planted you, has decreed disaster against you because of the sin of the house of Israel and the house of Judah, which they committed to provoke me to anger by offering incense to Baal.
11.18: The Lord informed me of it and I knew it, so you made their works known to me.
11.19: I was like a gentle lamb led to the slaughter, and I did not know that they were plotting against me: “Let us destroy the tree with its fruit. Let us cut it off from the land of the living, and let its name be remembered no more.”.
11.20: But the Lord of hosts judges with justice; he searches the hearts and minds. I will see the vengeance you will take on them, for to you I have entrusted my cause.
11.21: This is why the Lord says this about the men of Anathoth who want to kill you and who say: Do not prophesy in the name of the Lord, or you will die by our hand.
11.22: Therefore this is what the Lord of hosts says: I will punish them; the young men will die by the sword, their sons and daughters will die of hunger.
11.23: None of them will escape, for I will bring disaster upon the men of Anathoth in the year I visit them.

12.1: You are too just, O Lord, for me to argue with you; I only want to speak with you about justice: Why does the way of the wicked prosper, why do all the perfidious live in peace?
12.2: You plant them and they take root, they grow and bear fruit; you are near their mouths but far from their hearts.
12.3: And you, Lord, know me, you see me, and you know what my heart is for you. Take them away like sheep, to the slaughter; consign them to the day of slaughter.
12.4: How long will the land mourn and the grass of every field wither? Because of the wickedness of those who dwell in it, livestock and birds perish, for they say, “He will not see our end.”.
12.5: If you run with foot soldiers and they tire you out, how will you fight with horsemen? If you need a land of peace to have confidence, what will you do against the lions of the Jordan?
12.6: For even your brothers and your father’s house are betraying you; they are shouting loudly behind you. Do not trust them when they speak good words to you.
12.7: I left my home, I abandoned my inheritance, I delivered the object of my love into the hands of my enemies.
12.8: My heritage has become like a lion in the forest to me; it has raised its voice against me, so I have come to hate it.
12.9: Is my inheritance a mottled vulture, against which vultures swoop from all sides? Come, gather all the beasts of the field, bring them to the feast.
12.10: Many shepherds have destroyed my vineyard, trampled my land underfoot, they have turned the lot that was dear to me into a desert, into devastation.
12.11: They have made it a ruin, devastated, he is in mourning before me, the whole country is ravaged, because no one has taken it to heart.
12.12: On all the hills of the desert come the destroyers, for the Lord has a devouring sword; from one end of the land to the other, there is no salvation for all flesh.
12.13: They sowed wheat, they reap thorns; they spent their strength, but without profit. Be ashamed of what you harvest: this is the effect of the Lord's fierce anger.
12.14: This is what the Lord says about all my wicked neighbors who attack the inheritance I gave to my people Israel: I am going to uproot them from their land and I will uproot the house of Judah from among them.
12.15: But after I have uprooted them, I will again have compassion on them and I will bring them back, each to their inheritance, each to their own country.
12.16: And if they learn the ways of my people, swearing by my name, "As the Lord lives," just as they taught my people to swear by Baal, they shall be established among my people.
12.17: But if they do not listen, I will uproot this nation, I will uproot it and destroy it. This is the Lord’s declaration.

13.1: This is what the Lord said to me: Go and buy yourself a linen belt and put it around your waist, but do not put it in the water.
13.2: And I bought myself the belt, according to the word of the Lord, and I put it around my waist.
13.3: The word of the Lord came to me a second time, in these words:
13.4: Take the belt you bought and that is around your waist, get up, go towards the Euphrates and there you will hide it in a crevice in the rock.
13.5: I went and hid her near the Euphrates, as the Lord had commanded me.
13.6: And after many days the Lord said to me, “Get up, go to the Euphrates and there retrieve the belt that I commanded you to hide there.”
13.7: I went to the Euphrates, I dug and I retrieved the belt from the place where I had hidden it, and there it was, the belt was lost, it was no longer good for anything.
13.8: And the word of the Lord came to me, saying:
13.9: Thus says the Lord: In this way I will destroy the pride of Judah and the great pride of Jerusalem.
13.10: This wicked people who refuse to listen to my words, who follow the stubbornness of their hearts and who go after other gods to serve and worship them, they will be like this belt, which is no longer good for anything.
13.11: For, as a belt is fastened around a man’s waist, so I had fastened all the house of Israel and all the house of Judah to myself, declares the Lord, that they might be to me a people, a name, an honor, and a glory; but they did not listen.
13.12: And you shall say to them this word: Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel: Every jar must be filled with wine. They will answer you: Do we not know that every jar must be filled with wine?
13.13: And you shall say to them: Thus says the Lord: I will fill with drunkenness all the inhabitants of this land and the kings who sit on the throne of David, the priests and the prophets and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem.
13.14: And I will break them one against another, fathers and sons together. This is the Lord’s declaration: I will not spare, I will not have compassion, I will not have pity, lest I destroy them.
13.15: Listen, pay attention, do not be proud, for the Lord has spoken.
13.16: Give glory to the Lord your God before darkness comes, before your feet stumble on the mountains of night, before he turns the light you expect into a shadow of death and makes it deep darkness.
13.17: If you do not listen to this, my soul will weep in secret because of your pride, my eyes will weep bitterly, overflow with tears, for Jacob's flock will be taken captive.
13.18: Tell the king and queen: Sit on the ground, for your crown of glory has fallen from your head.
13.19: The cities of the south are closed and no one opens them, all of Judah is deported, the deportation is complete.
13.20: Lift up your eyes and see those who are coming from the north: Where is the flock that was given to you, the sheep that were your glory?
13.21: What will you say when the Lord gives you as masters those whom you have instructed against you, your close associates? Will you not be seized by pains, like a woman in childbirth?
13.22: And if you say in your heart, "Why have these misfortunes befallen me?" It is because of the multitude of your iniquities that the skirts of your robe have been lifted up, that your heels have been bruised.
13.23: Will an Ethiopian change his skin, a leopard its spots? And you, could you do good, you who are used to doing evil?
13.24: I will scatter them like chaff that blows away, before the breath of the desert wind.
13.25: This is your fate, the portion I have measured out to you, declares the Lord, because you have forgotten me and trusted in lies.
13.26: And I too will lift the folds of your dress over your face and your shame will be seen.
13.27: Your adulteries, your neighing, your shameless prostitution on the hills in the open country—all your abominations I have seen. Woe to you, Jerusalem! How long will you remain unclean?

14.1: Word of the Lord which was addressed to Jeremiah on the occasion of the drought.
14.2: Judah is in mourning, its gates languish, they lie desolate on the ground, and the cry of Jerusalem rises.
14.3: The older ones send the younger ones to fetch water, they go to the cisterns, they do not find any water, they return with their empty vessels, they are confused and ashamed, they cover their heads.
14.4: Because of the cracked ground, because there has been no rain on the land, the plowmen are confused, they cover their heads.
14.5: Even the doe in the countryside gives birth and abandons her young, because there is no grass.
14.6: The onagers stand on the heights, sucking in the air like jackals, their eyes going dull, because there is no greenery.
14.7: If our iniquities testify against us, Lord, act for the honor of your name, for our unfaithfulness is many, we have sinned against you.
14.8: O you, the hope of Israel, its deliverer in times of distress, why should you be like a stranger in the land, like a traveler who pitches his tent there for the night?
14.9: Why should you be like a man in despair, like a hero powerless to deliver? Yet you dwell in our midst, Lord, your name is invoked upon us, do not abandon us.
14.10: This is what the Lord says about this people: They love to wander about and do not restrain their feet. The Lord no longer takes pleasure in them. He will now remember their iniquities and punish their sins.
14.11: And the Lord said to me: Do not intercede on behalf of this people.
14.12: When they fast, I will not listen to their pleas; when they offer me burnt offerings and grain offerings, I will not accept them, for I will destroy them with the sword, famine, and plague.
14.13: And I answered: Ah Lord God, behold the prophets say to them: You shall not see the sword and you shall not have famine, but I will give you sure peace in this place.
14.14: And the Lord said to me, “The prophets are prophesying lies in my name. I have not sent them, nor have I commanded them, nor have I spoken to them. They are prophesying to you false visions, vain divinations, and deceit from their own hearts.”.
14.15: Therefore this is what the Lord says: Concerning the prophets who prophesy in my name, though I have not sent them, and who say, “There will be no sword or famine in this land,” those prophets will surely perish by the sword and by famine.
14.16: And the people to whom they prophesy will be thrown into the streets of Jerusalem, victims of famine and sword, and there will be no one to bury them, their wives, their sons and their daughters, and I will pour out on them their wickedness.
14.17: And you shall say to them this word: My eyes will overflow with tears night and day, without ceasing, for the virgin, daughter of my people, will be struck by a great disaster, by a very painful wound.
14.18: If I go out into the fields, behold, men slain by the sword; if I enter the city, behold, pangs of famine. Even the prophet and the priest wander, toward a land they did not know.
14.19: Have you utterly rejected Judah? Has your soul loathed Zion? Why have you struck us, and there is no healing for us? We looked for peace, but no good has come; for a time of healing, but behold, terror.
14.20: We acknowledge, O Lord, our wickedness, the iniquity of our fathers, for we have sinned against you.
14.21: For your name's sake, do not despise, do not profane your glorious throne; remember, do not break your covenant with us.
14.22: Among the worthless idols of the nations, is there any that can bring rain? Can the heavens send down showers? Are you not, Lord, our God? We hope in you, for it is you who do all these things.

15.1: The Lord answered me: Even if Moses and Samuel were to stand before me, my soul would not turn toward this people; drive them out from before my face and let them depart.
15.2: And if they ask you, “Where shall we go?” you shall say to them, “Thus says the Lord: He who is destined for death, to death; he who is destined for the sword, to the sword; he who is destined for famine, to famine; and he who is destined for captivity, to captivity.”.
15.3: And I will raise up against them four kinds of plagues, declares the Lord: the sword to kill, the dogs to tear, the birds of the air and the beasts of the earth to devour and destroy.
15.4: I will make it an object of horror to all the kingdoms of the earth, because of Manasseh, son of Hezekiah, king of Judah, for what he did in Jerusalem.
15.5: Who will have pity on you, Jerusalem? Who will mourn for you? Who will turn aside from their way to inquire about your condition?
15.6: You have rejected me, oracle of the Lord, to withdraw you, and I will stretch out my hand against you to destroy you; I am weary of showing mercy.
15.7: I will winnow them with the winnowing fork at the gates of the land, I will deprive them of children, I will destroy my people, they do not turn from their ways.
15.8: His widows will be more numerous than the sands of the sea. I will bring them, upon the mother of the young warrior, the destroyer at midday, I will suddenly unleash upon her anguish and terror.
15.9: The mother of the seven sons feels faint; she is about to die. Her sun sets while it is still day; she is ashamed and disgraced. Those who remain, I will give up to the sword before their enemies, declares the Lord.
15.10: Woe to me, O my mother, because you bore me, to be a man of strife and contention to all the land. I have lent nothing and they have lent me nothing, and everyone curses me.
15.11: The Lord says: Yes, I will strengthen you for your own good; I will surely cause your enemy to beg you in times of trouble and distress.
15.12: Will iron break the iron of the north and the bronze?
15.13: I will give your possessions and treasures up for plunder, without payment, for all your sins and throughout your territory
15.14: and I will make them pass with your enemies into a land you do not know, for a fire has been kindled in my anger, and it will burn upon you.
15.15: You know, Lord, remember me, take care of me and avenge me on my persecutors, do not take me away, in your patience towards them, know that it is for you that I bear the reproach.
15.16: As soon as your words came, I devoured them; they became my joy and the gladness of my heart, for your name was invoked upon me, Lord, God of hosts.
15.17: I did not sit in the assembly of laughers, to indulge in gaiety, under your hand, I sat alone, for you had filled me with wrath.
15.18: Why is my suffering endless and my painful wound resistant to healing? Are you to me like a deceptive stream, like waters that cannot be relied upon?
15.19: This is why the Lord says: If you return to me, I will restore you to stand in my presence; if you separate the precious from the worthless, you will be like my mouth; they will return to you, but you will not return to them.
15.20: I will make you a strong wall of bronze for this people; they will fight against you, but they will not prevail against you, for I will be with you to help you and deliver you, declares the Lord.
15.21: I will deliver you from the hand of the wicked and I will redeem you from the hand of the violent.

16.1: The word of the Lord came to me in these terms:
16.2: You shall not take a wife and you shall not have sons or daughters in this place.
16.3: For thus says the Lord concerning the sons and daughters who are born in this place, and concerning the mothers who bear them, and concerning the fathers who beget them, in this land:
16.4: They will die of deadly diseases; they will not be given tears or burial, they will be like dung on the ground. They will perish by the sword and famine, and their corpses will be food for the birds of the air and the beasts of the earth.
16.5: For thus says the Lord: Do not enter the house of mourning, do not go and weep and lament with them, for I have withdrawn my peace from this people, declares the Lord, my grace and my compassion.
16.6: Great and small will die in this country, there will be no burial for them nor tears, no cuttings will be made, no shaving will be done for them.
16.7: They will not be given the bread of mourning to comfort them concerning the dead, nor will they be given the cup of consolation to drink for a father and a mother.
16.8: Do not go into the house of the feast to sit with them, to eat and drink.
16.9: For thus says the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel: I will put an end in this place, before your very eyes and in your days, to the sound of joy and the sound of gladness, the song of bridegroom and the song of bride.
16.10: When you tell these people all these things, they will say to you, “Why does the Lord tell us all these great calamities? What is our iniquity and what sin have we committed against the Lord our God?”
16.11: And you shall say to them: It is because your fathers forsook me, declares the Lord, and went after other gods, and served and worshiped them, and forsook me, and did not observe my law.
16.12: And you have done more evil than your fathers, and now each of you goes after the perversity of your evil heart, refusing to listen to me.
16.13: I will drive you out of this land into a land neither you nor your ancestors have known, and there you will serve foreign gods night and day, for I will show you no mercy.
16.14: Therefore, behold, the days are coming, declares the Lord, when it will no longer be said: “As surely as the Lord lives, who brought the children of Israel up out of the land of Egypt.”
16.15: But: the Lord lives, he who brought the children of Israel up from the land of the north and from all the countries where he had banished them. And I will bring them back to their land which I gave to their fathers.
16.16: “Behold, I will call a multitude of fishermen,” declares the Lord, “and they will catch them. And after that, I will call a multitude of hunters, and they will hunt them down from every mountain and hill and from the clefts of the rocks.”.
16.17: For my eyes are on all their ways; they are not hidden from my face, nor is their iniquity concealed from my eyes.
16.18: First, I will pay them double for their iniquity and their sin, because they have defiled my land; they have filled my inheritance with the corpses of their idols and their abominations.
16.19: Lord, my strength, my fortress and my refuge in the day of distress, the nations will come to you from the ends of the earth and say: Our fathers inherited nothing but lies, worthless vanities.
16.20: Is it possible for a man to create gods for himself? And they are not gods.
16.21: Therefore, behold, I will make known to them, this time, I will make known to them my hand and my power, and they will know that my name is the Lord.

17.1: The sin of Judah is written with an iron stylus, with a diamond point; it is engraved on the tablet of their hearts and on the horns of your altars.
17.2: As they remember their children, so they remember their altars and their Asherahs, near the green trees, on the high hills.
17.3: O my mountain that is in the plain, I will give up your possessions to plunder, all your treasures, your high places, because of your sins throughout your territory.
17.4: You will leave fallow, and through your own fault, the inheritance I gave you; I will make you serve your enemies in a land you do not know, for you have kindled the fire of my anger, and it burns forever.
17.5: Thus says the Lord: Cursed is the man who trusts in man, who makes flesh his arm and whose heart departs from the Lord.
17.6: He is like a heather in the moor, he does not rejoice when happiness comes, he will occupy the scorched places in the desert, a salt land where no one lives.
17.7: Blessed is the man who trusts in the Lord and whose trust is the Lord.
17.8: It is like a tree planted by the water's edge, which pushes its roots towards the current; it does not fear when the heat comes and its leaves remain green; it does not worry about the year of drought and does not cease to bear fruit.
17.9: The heart is cunning above all things and corrupt; who can know it?
17.10: I, the Lord, search the hearts and test the minds, to reward each person according to their ways, according to the fruit of their deeds.
17.11: A partridge broods over eggs it did not lay, like one who acquires riches unjustly; in the middle of his days he must leave them and at his end he is but a fool.
17.12: Throne of glory, eternal height, place of our sanctuary,
17.13: Hope of Israel, Lord, all who forsake you will be put to shame. Those who turn away from me will be written in the dust, for they have forsaken the Lord, the spring of living water.
17.14: Heal me, Lord, and I shall be healed; save me, and I shall be saved, for you are my praise.
17.15: They are saying to me, 'Where is the word of the Lord? Let it come.'.
17.16: And I did not refuse to be a shepherd in your footsteps, I did not desire the day of misfortune, you know, what came out of my lips was present before your face.
17.17: Do not be a cause of ruin for me; you are my refuge in the day of trouble.
17.18: May my persecutors be put to shame, but I myself not be put to shame. May they tremble, but I not tremble. Bring upon them the day of calamity and shatter them with a double breach.
17.19: This is what the Lord said to me: “Go and stand at the gate of the people, through which the kings of Judah enter and exit, and at all the gates of Jerusalem.”
17.20: and you shall say to them: Hear the word of the Lord, you kings of Judah and all Judah and all you inhabitants of Jerusalem who enter through these gates.
17.21: Thus says the Lord: Take heed to your souls and do not carry burdens on the Sabbath day, nor bring them through the gates of Jerusalem.
17.22: Do not carry any burdens out of your houses on the Sabbath day, nor do any work. And keep the Sabbath day holy, as I commanded your fathers.
17.23: They did not listen or pay attention; they stiffened their necks so as not to listen and not to receive the instruction.
17.24: If you listen to me obediently, declares the Lord, by not bringing any burdens through the gates of this city on the Sabbath day, by keeping the Sabbath day holy by not doing any work on it,
17.25: Then through the gates of this city will enter kings and princes sitting on the throne of David, riding on chariots and horses, they and their princes, the men of Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and this city will be inhabited forever.
17.26: People will come from the towns of Judah and the surrounding areas of Jerusalem, from the land of Benjamin, from Shephelah, from the hill country and from the Negev, bringing burnt offerings and sacrifices, grain offerings and incense, and bringing peace offerings into the house of the Lord.
17.27: But if you do not listen to me by keeping the Sabbath day holy, by not carrying any burden when you enter the gates of Jerusalem on the Sabbath day, then I will kindle a fire at the gates of the city and it will consume the palaces of Jerusalem and it will not be quenched.

18.1: The word that came to Jeremiah from the Lord in these terms:
18.2: Get up and go down to the potter's house, and there I will make you hear my word.
18.3: I went down to the potter's house and there he was, working on the wheels.
18.4: And the vase he was making failed, as happens to clay in the potter's hand, and he made another vase, as it seemed good in the potter's eyes to do.
18.5: And the word of the Lord came to me in these words:
18.6: Can I not make for you as this potter made, O house of Israel, declares the Lord? Yes, as clay is in the potter's hand, so are you in my hand, O house of Israel.
18.7: Sometimes I speak, concerning a nation and concerning a kingdom, of uprooting, tearing down, and destroying.
18.8: But if this nation, against which I have spoken, turns from its wickedness, then I relent of the harm I had resolved to do to it.
18.9: Sometimes I speak, concerning a nation and concerning a kingdom, of building and planting.
18.10: But if this nation does what is wrong in my eyes, by not listening to my voice, then I repent of the good that I said I would do to it.
18.11: And now speak to the men of Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem, saying: Thus says the Lord: Behold, I am plotting disaster against you and devising a plan against you. Therefore turn, each of you, from your evil ways and reform your ways and your deeds.
18.12: But they say: It is in vain. We will follow our thoughts, we will each act according to the obstinacy of our evil heart.
18.13: Therefore this is what the Lord says: Ask the nations: Who has ever heard of such a thing? The virgin of Israel has committed horrible abominations.
18.14: Does she leave the rock of the plain, the snow of Lebanon? Do we see the waters that come from afar, fresh and flowing, dry up?
18.15: But my people have forgotten me; they offer incense to nothingness. Idols make them stumble in their ways, the paths of old, to follow trails, an untrodden road.,
18.16: to make their country a desolation, an object of eternal mockery, all those who pass through will be astonished and shake their heads.
18.17: Like the east wind, I will scatter them before the enemy; I will show them my back and not my face, on the day of their disaster.
18.18: And they said, “Come, let us plot against Jeremiah, for the law will not perish with the priest, nor counsel with the wise, nor the word of God with the prophet. Come, let us strike him with our tongues and pay no attention to any of his words.”.
18.19: Give me your ear, Lord, and hear the voice of my adversaries.
18.20: Will evil be repaid for good, that they dig a pit for my soul? Remember how I stood before you, to speak to you on their behalf, to turn away your anger from them.
18.21: Therefore, give their children over to famine and abandon them to the edge of the sword. Let their wives lose their children and their husbands, let their men die by plague, and let their young men be struck down by the sword in battle.
18.22: Let their cries be heard from their homes when you suddenly bring armed bands against them. For they have dug a pit to capture me and have hidden nets before my feet.
18.23: And you, Lord, know all their plots to kill me. Do not forgive their iniquity, do not blot out their sin from your sight; let them bow down before you; deal with them in the time of your anger.

19.1: Thus says the Lord: Go, buy yourself a potter's jug, and take with you some of the elders of the people and some of the elders of the priests.
19.2: And go out to the Valley of Ben Hinnom, which is at the entrance of the Potsherd Gate, and there proclaim the words that I tell you.
19.3: You will say: Hear the word of the Lord, kings of Judah and inhabitants of Jerusalem. Thus says the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel: Behold, I am bringing disaster upon this place, such that the ears of all who hear of it will ring,
19.4: because they have abandoned me, alienated this place, offered incense to foreign gods that neither they, nor their fathers, nor the kings of Judah knew, and filled this place with the blood of the innocent.
19.5: They built the high places of Baal, to consume their children by fire as a burnt offering to Baal, things which I had neither commanded nor spoken of, nor which had not entered my heart.
19.6: Therefore, behold, the days are coming, declares the Lord, when this place will no longer be called Topheth or Valley of Ben Hinnom, but Valley of Slaughter.
19.7: I will render vain in this place the counsel of Judah and Jerusalem, I will cause them to fall by the sword before their enemies and by the hand of those who seek their lives, and I will give their corpses as food to the birds of the air and the beasts of the earth.
19.8: And I will make this city an object of astonishment and derision; everyone who passes by it will be astonished and will laugh at all its bruises.
19.9: I will make them eat the flesh of their sons and the flesh of their daughters; they will eat the flesh of one another, in the anguish and distress to which their enemies and those who seek their lives will reduce them.
19.10: You will then break the jug in front of the men who came with you.,
19.11: and you shall say to them: Thus says the Lord of hosts: I will break this people and this city, as one breaks a potter's vessel, which can no longer be repaired, and they shall be buried in Topheth, for there is no place to bury them.
19.12: This is what I will do to this place, declares the Lord, and to its inhabitants, by making this city like Topheth.
19.13: The houses of Jerusalem and the houses of the kings of Judah will be like this place of Topheth, defiled: all the houses on whose roofs incense has been offered to all the host of heaven and libations poured out to foreign gods.
19.14: Jeremiah returned from Topheth where the Lord had sent him to prophesy, and standing in the courtyard of the house of the Lord, he said to all the people:
19.15: Thus says the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel: Behold, I am going to bring upon this city and upon all the cities around it all the disasters that I have announced against it, because they have stiffened their necks in not obeying my words.

20.1: Phassur the priest, son of Emmer, who was chief overseer in the house of the Lord, heard Jeremiah utter these prophecies.
20.2: And Phassur struck Jeremiah the prophet and put him in the stocks at the Upper Gate of Benjamin, which is in the house of the Lord.
20.3: The next day, Phassur released Jeremiah from the yoke, and Jeremiah said to him, "The Lord no longer calls you Phassur, but Magor-Missabib."
20.4: For thus says the Lord: Behold, I will deliver you and all your friends to terror; they shall fall by the sword of their enemies, and your eyes shall see it. And I will also deliver all Judah into the hand of the king of Babylon, and he shall carry them off captive to Babylon and strike them with the sword.
20.5: I will deliver all the riches of this city, all its produce, all its precious objects and all the treasures of the kings of Judah, I will deliver them into the hands of their enemies and they will plunder them, take them away and carry them off to Babylon.
20.6: And you, Phassur, and all who dwell in your house, will go into captivity; you will go to Babylon and there you will die, there you will be buried, you and all your friends, to whom you prophesied lies.
20.7: You have seduced me, Lord, and I have been seduced; you have seized me and prevailed. I am a laughingstock all day long; everyone mocks me.
20.8: For every time I speak, I cry out, I announce violence and devastation, and the word of the Lord is for me reproach and derision all day long.
20.9: I said, "I will no longer mention him, I will no longer speak in his name." There was something like a consuming fire in my heart, shut up in my bones; I tried to contain it, but I could not.
20.10: For I heard the wicked talk of the crowd: Terror on all sides. Denounce him, let us go and denounce him. All those with whom I was at peace are watching my steps: If he allows himself to be seduced, we will prevail against him and take revenge on him.
20.11: But the Lord is with me like a mighty warrior; therefore my persecutors will stumble and not prevail. They will be ashamed of their failure, of an everlasting disgrace that will not be forgotten.
20.12: Lord of hosts, you who test the righteous, who see the hearts and minds, I will see the vengeance you will take from them, for to you I have entrusted my cause.
20.13: Sing to the Lord, praise the Lord, for he has rescued the soul of the afflicted from the hand of the wicked.
20.14: Cursed be the day I was born. May the day my mother gave birth to me not be blessed.
20.15: Cursed be the man who brought the news to my father, saying, "A male child is born to you," and he was filled with joy.
20.16: May this man be like the cities that the Lord overthrew without repenting. May he hear in the morning the cry of the vanquished and at midday the shouts of the victors.
20.17: Because he did not kill me from the womb, so that my mother might be my tomb, or that her womb might keep me forever.
20.18: Why did I come forth from her womb, to see pain and sorrow and to waste my days in ignominy?

21.1: The word that came to Jeremiah from the Lord, when King Zedekiah sent to him Phassur son of Melkiah and the priest Zephaniah son of Maasiah, to tell him:
21.2: Please consult the Lord for us, for Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, is waging war against us; perhaps the Lord will renew all his great miracles in our favor, so that he will depart from us.
21.3: Jeremiah answered them: This is what you are to say to Zedekiah:
21.4: Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel: Behold, I will turn back the weapons of war in your hands, with which you fight outside the walls against the king of Babylon and the Chaldeans who are besieging you, and I will gather them in the midst of the city,
21.5: And I will fight against you with an outstretched hand and a mighty arm, with anger, fury, and great indignation.
21.6: I will strike the inhabitants of this city, both men and beasts, and they will die of a great plague.
21.7: After this, declares the Lord, I will deliver Zedekiah king of Judah, his servants, the people, and those in this city who have escaped from the plague, the sword, and the famine, into the hands of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, into the hands of their enemies and those who seek their lives, and he will put them to the edge of the sword; he will not spare them, he will have neither pity nor compassion.
21.8: And you shall say to this people: Thus says the Lord: Behold, I set before you the way of life and the way of death.
21.9: Whoever remains in this city will die by the sword, by famine, or by plague; whoever leaves it to surrender to the Chaldeans who are besieging you will live and have his life as plunder.
21.10: For I have turned my face toward this city to harm it and not to help it, declares the Lord, it will be given into the hands of the king of Babylon and he will burn it down.
21.11: And to the house of the king of Judah you shall say: Hear the word of the Lord,
